name: Holly Clusters
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Place sheets of foil on countertop for cooling cookies.
2. Combine brown sugar, shortening, milk and vanilla in large bowl. Beat at medium speed of electric mixer until well blended. Add egg into creamed mixture.
3. Gradually add flour, salt and baking soda, mixing just until combined. Stir in chocolate chips and pecans.
4. Using a rounded tablespoon (15 mL), drop dough 3 inches (7 cm) apart onto ungreased baking sheets.
5. Bake one baking sheet at a time for 8 to 10 minutes for chewy cookies, or 11 to 13 minutes for crisp cookies. Do not over bake. Cool on baking sheet for 2 minutes before transferring to foil to cool completely.
6. For decoration, mix icing sugar with water to form thick paste.
7. Add green food coloring to marzipan until desired color. Roll into thin strips (1/16-inch or 2 mm) and cut into holly leaf shapes using a sharp knife or small cutter. Place leaves on cooled cookies with the paste. Add small red candies for holly berries. Allow decorations to set completely before storing cookies. Approximately 3 dozen cookies.
If nuts are omitted, add an additional 1/2 cup (125 mL) semi-sweet chocolate chips.
